nodes.title: Golden Hour
nodes.body_text:
Here's a full summary of the VideoNode:

## VideoNode — Functions & Features

### Playback Engine
- Uses **QMediaPlayer + QAudioOutput + QVideoSink** for native video decoding
- Supports `.mp4`, `.avi`, `.mov`, `.mkv`, `.webm`, `.wmv`, `.flv`, `.m4v`
- **Play/pause toggle** — double-click the video area or press **Space** when selected
- **Stop** — stops playback entirely
- **Loop toggle** — replays from the start when the video reaches the end
- **Mute toggle** — silences audio output without changing volume level
- **Volume** — stored as 0–100 integer, persisted across sessions

### Progress / Scrub Bar
- Rendered below the video frame as a rounded bar with fill ratio
- **Click to seek** — pauses during scrub, resumes on mouse release
- **Drag to scrub** — continuous seeking while dragging across the bar
- Displays current position vs. duration internally (has `_fmt_time` helper for `m:ss` formatting)

### Video Frame Rendering
- Each decoded frame is **immediately scaled down** to the display rect size — full-res frames are never kept in memory
- **Frame-skip throttle** — if the previous frame hasn't been painted yet, incoming frames are dropped
- **Scaled cache** — re-uses the last scaled pixmap until the node is resized
- Frames are clipped to a **rounded rectangle** with a subtle **bevel border** (dark outer + light inner line)
- When paused, a **semi-transparent play triangle** overlay appears centered on the video

### Loading & File Browser
- **Double-click** an empty video area to open a native file dialog
- **Drag-and-drop** from Explorer — handled by `IntricateView.dropEvent`, which calls `load_from_path()`
- Remembers the **last browsed directory** in `settings.toml` under `[node.video.last_dir]`
- On load, auto-sets the caption to the file stem and **spawns a connected AboutNode** below with that caption

### Button Row
- Starts **hidden** — double-click the top strip above the video to reveal/hide
- Includes inherited base buttons plus **Loop** and **Mute** toggle buttons with on/off icon states

### Viewport Culling
- When the node scrolls **off-screen**, playback is automatically **paused**
- When it scrolls **back into view**, playback **resumes** if it was playing before

### Session Persistence (VideoNodeData)
- Persists: `source_path`, `caption`, `volume`, `playback_pos`, `looping`, `muted`
- On save, snapshots the current playback position in milliseconds
- On restore, **deferred seek** — waits for `LoadedMedia` status, then seeks to the saved position and grabs one frame so the node isn't blank
- Videos are **never embedded** — only the file path is stored; missing files show a dashed placeholder

### Lifecycle / Cleanup
- `_prepare_for_removal()` stops the player, disconnects all four signal connections, and nulls out pixmap caches to prevent reference cycles and post-destruction callbacks
- Guards all signal callbacks with a `_destroyed` flag + `RuntimeError` catches for safety against C++ object deletion races

nodes.title: Text
nodes.label:
# Intricate

**Intricate** is a frameless, always-on-top node-based visual canvas built with PySide6. You place nodes on an infinite zoomable canvas, connect them via ports, and arrange your thoughts spatially rather than linearly. It is part of a "Single Shared Braincell" family of apps that share live configuration via a common `settings.toml` file and a set of environment variables.

Current node types: **WarmNode** (free-form text with emoji accent), **HealthNode** (live system monitor — GC census and OS-level click tracking), **BezierNode** (interactive bezier curve with draggable control handles), **ImageNode** (drag-and-drop or browse images with editable caption), and **AboutNode** (sticky note for labelling groups of nodes).

The canvas supports cursor-anchored zoom, middle-mouse pan, and drag-and-drop of image files directly from Explorer. Nodes have ambient hover pulse animations driven by a separate `NodeBehaviour` personality system. The entire theme — colors, icons, layout — hot-reloads the moment `settings.toml` is saved by any app in the family.

## Architecture

```
📄 main.py
└── 📄 main_window.py (IntricateApp — QMainWindow)
      ├── 📁 graphics/
      │     ├── 📄 Scene.py          — IntricateScene, canvas and node factory
      │     ├── 📄 View.py           — IntricateView, pan / zoom / drag-drop
      │     └── 📄 Theme.py          — all visual values, icons, live reload
      ├── 📁 nodes/
      │     ├── 📄 BaseNode.py       — base class every node builds on
      │     ├── 📄 NodeBehaviour.py  — hover pulse and personality system
      │     ├── 📄 NodeButton.py     — node-embedded controls
      │     ├── 📄 Port.py           — connection ports
      │     ├── 📄 WarmNode.py       — text + emoji content node
      │     ├── 📄 HealthNode.py     — live system monitor node
      │     ├── 📄 BezierNode.py     — bezier curve node
      │     ├── 📄 ImageNode.py      — image display node
      │     └── 📄 AboutNode.py      — sticky note node
      ├── 📁 data/
      │     ├── 📄 NodeData.py       — base dataclass, no Qt
      │     └── 📄 XxxNodeData.py    — one per node type, state + serialization
      ├── 📁 widgets/
      │     ├── 📄 NoteEditor.py     — text editor dialog
      │     └── 📄 PrettyButton.py   — themed button
      └── 📁 utils/
            ├── 📄 settings.py       — TOML loader + file watcher
            ├── 📄 logger.py         — 3-slot rotating log
            ├── 📄 vision.py         — image processing
            └── 📄 OSClickMonitor.py — global Windows mouse hook
```

---
*It is what it is*
